messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following fitness tracking app privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"FitPulse tracks your steps, heart rate, sleep patterns, and GPS location to provide personalised fitness insights. We share anonymised aggregate data with research partners. Your data is stored on servers in the US. You can delete your account at any time."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated for any processing activity.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [FAIL] Heart rate and sleep data constitute health data under Art. 9. No explicit consent or other Art. 9(2) basis is stated for processing this special category data.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] "Personalised fitness insights" is stated as primary purpose. Research partner sharing is a secondary purpose not clearly consented to.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] GPS location collection for fitness insights requires justification — it may be disproportionate depending on feature set.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [FAIL] No retention period stated. Account deletion is mentioned but no timeframe for actual data erasure is given.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] Only account deletion (erasure) is implied. No rights to access, rectification, portability, restriction, or objection are mentioned.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] Research partners are referenced but not named. No DPA or processor relationship is described.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] "Servers in the US" is disclosed but no transfer safeguard mechanism is stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Large-scale health data processing likely requires a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 9 (health data processed without explicit consent), Art. 5 (no retention period, GPS proportionality unjustified), Art. 15-21 (rights absent), Art. 28 (research partners undisclosed), Art. 46 (US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO likely required)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Obtain and document explicit consent separately for health data (Art. 9)
- State lawful basis for each processing activity
- Justify GPS collection or make it optional
- Define a retention period and erasure timeline post-account deletion
- List all data subject rights with a contact mechanism
- Name research partners and confirm anonymisation meets GDPR standards
- Reference US transfer safeguards (SCCs or adequacy)
- Appoint a DPO given scale of health data processing
